meta.name: AQ-10 Autism Spectrum Quotient
meta.description: The AQ-10 is a brief screening version of the Autism Spectrum Quotient (AQ-50), consisting of 10 items designed to quickly identify individuals who may need further assessment for autism spectrum traits. It evaluates social skills, attention switching, attention to detail, communication, and imagination.
meta.author: Allison, Auyeung & Baron-Cohen (2012)
meta.reference: Allison C, Auyeung B, Baron-Cohen S. Toward brief "Red Flags" for autism screening: The Short Autism Spectrum Quotient and the Short Quantitative Checklist for Autism in toddlers in 1,000 cases and 3,000 controls. J Am Acad Child Adolesc Psychiatry. 2012;51(2):202-212.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement below and select the option that best describes you.

questions.text: I often notice small sounds when others do not
I usually concentrate more on the whole picture, rather than the small details
I find it easy to do more than one thing at once
If there is an interruption, I can switch back to what I was doing very quickly
I find it easy to 'read between the lines' when someone is talking to me
I know how to tell if someone listening to me is getting bored
When I'm reading a story I find it difficult to work out the characters' intentions
I like to collect information about categories of things (e.g. types of car, types of bird, types of train, types of plant, etc.)
I find it easy to work out what someone is thinking or feeling just by looking at their face
I find it difficult to work out people's intentions

scoring.description: For agree items, score 1 if Definitely Agree or Slightly Agree; for disagree items, score 1 if Slightly Disagree or Definitely Disagree

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range, showing no significant autism spectrum traits. This indicates that the relevant behavioral characteristics are within the normal range with no significant functional impact.
suggestions: This result suggests you are unlikely to be on the autism spectrum
If you still have concerns, consult a professional for a more comprehensive evaluation
Understand your behavioral characteristics and leverage your strengths
Maintain your current effective coping strategies
Seek information and support when needed

ranges.label: Borderline Range
ranges.description: Your score is in the borderline range, suggesting some possible autism spectrum traits. Mild behavioral differences are common. Understanding your own patterns can help you adapt more effectively to different environments.
suggestions: This score does not imply a diagnosis, but may warrant further attention
If these traits affect your daily life, consider consulting a professional
Learning about autism spectrum conditions can help with self-understanding
Learn about your behavioral patterns and characteristics
Use planners and reminders to help organize daily activities

ranges.label: Further Assessment Recommended
ranges.description: Your score is elevated, suggesting possible autism spectrum traits. A more comprehensive professional assessment is recommended. At this level, behavioral characteristics may have some impact on learning, work, or social interactions. Understanding and adapting strategies can be helpful.
suggestions: Consider contacting a professional for a comprehensive autism spectrum assessment
This screening result is not a diagnosis and requires professional clinical evaluation
Regardless of the outcome, understanding your characteristics helps develop suitable coping strategies
Look for local autism support resources and communities
Consider a more comprehensive professional assessment
